entries.description: Operator confirmed via AskUserQuestion all three open questions per Claude's recommended answers: Q1 implementation lives in this repo (dabbler-ai-orchestration); Q2 narrower V1 categories list (refactor-placement, file-layout, scoping, spec-clarification); Q3 committed JSONL journal + gitignored consensus-decisions/ full-payload directory. Set-level NTE budget confirmed at $5. Answers recorded in ai-assignment.md for Session 2 reference.

Extended ai_router/router-config.yaml `delegation:` block with the new `decision_consensus` sub-block: enabled (default false), engines (default openai:gpt-5-4 + google:gemini-pro), V1 four-category list, unresolved_action enum (default ask_user), journal_path default (ai_router/consensus-decisions.jsonl), journal_full_payloads_dir default (ai_router/consensus-decisions). Inline comments cross-reference the workflow doc + design proposal.

Added `_validate_decision_consensus(config)` to ai_router/config.py invoked at load_config boundary alongside the existing tier_assignments check. Validates: enabled is bool; engines is list of 'provider:model' strings with each model resolvable in config['models'] AND named provider matching the model's registered provider; categories from the recognized V1+V1.5+V2 whitelist (forward-compat for opt-in expansion); unresolved_action ∈ {ask_user, proceed_with_orchestrator_judgment}; journal_path and journal_full_payloads_dir are strings or null. Unknown sub-keys tolerated for forward-compat. Default-opt-out: absent block passes silently. Mirrored in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/configEditor/schemaValidator.ts (AJV) so the visual config editor accepts the same shape.

New module ai_router/consensus_journal.py (322 LOC). ConsensusRecord frozen dataclass mirrors the per-line shape from docs/planning/delegation-consensus-config.md. compute_question_hash returns sha256:-prefixed digest over (question_summary || category || timestamp); short_hash_from_full extracts the 6-char prefix for filenames. append_record opens journal_path in append mode, writes single JSON line, flushes, best-effort fsync (mirrors metrics.py precedent — POSIX append is atomic for sub-PIPE_BUF writes so no temp+rename required). write_full_payload uses temp+rename via os.replace for the Markdown sibling. write_consensus_record one-shot helper bundles both writes. validate_record_inputs guards the agreement_level + fallback_action enums. Module-level AGREEMENT_LEVELS and FALLBACK_ACTIONS tuples exposed so callers can import canonical names.

Two new test files: ai_router/tests/test_decision_consensus_schema.py (17 cases covering absent block, default shape, null payload dir, enabled-bool guard, engine 'provider:model' shape, unknown model, provider mismatch, V1.5/V2 forward-compat categories, unresolved_action enum, path-type guards, forward-compat unknown sub-keys) and ai_router/tests/test_consensus_journal.py (16 cases covering hash determinism + sensitivity, short-hash extraction + prefix guard, JSONL append + multi-line preservation + parent-dir creation, extras-merge with core-field protection, full-payload filename safety + content round-trip + no-leftover-temp, write_consensus_record optional-payload-dir logic, enum guards). Full ai_router suite at 633 passing + 1 skipped (was 599 at Set 030 close).

Added new top-level `## Decision-time consensus` section to docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md (160 lines) between `## Delegation Discipline` and `## Rules (Apply to All Orchestrators)`. Contents: framing paragraph contrasting task delegation vs decision delegation; six-step decision tree (human-only filter → category whitelist → parallel route → synthesis → apply-or-fallback → journal write); V1 vs human-only category examples table covering all four V1 slugs; journal-format example record (JSON code block); opt-in path (3-step); three explicit limits-of-consensus guardrails (human-only categories, synthesis discipline, auditable journal).

Round-A end-of-session verification: ai_router.query(model='gemini-pro', task_type='session-verification', content=bundle). Bundle size 43,764 bytes (router-config.yaml slice + _validate_decision_consensus + full consensus_journal.py + both new test files + AJV mirror slice + new workflow-doc section). Response: 1,924 bytes, one Critical finding. Cost $0.020. Per memory feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling — RouteResult dumped to JSON at c:\tmp\verify-031-s1\result.json before attribute access.

Round-A Critical finding addressed: a model entry in config['models'] missing a 'provider' key would silently pass `_validate_decision_consensus`'s engine-mismatch check because the comparison short-circuited on empty model_provider. Fixed by adding an explicit `if not model_provider: raise` guard between the unknown-model and mismatch checks in ai_router/config.py. Regression test `test_engine_provider_missing_in_model_rejected` added to test_decision_consensus_schema.py. Full ai_router suite re-run: 33/33 of the new tests pass.

Byte-identical 'Decision-time consensus (pointer)' section added to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and GEMINI.md in this repo. Diff-verified identical across all three files via grep -A 8. Inserts after the existing 'Delegation Discipline (pointer)' section in AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md, and after the 'Close-out and outsource-last' section in CLAUDE.md (which lacks a Delegation Discipline pointer per existing divergence).

ai_router/CHANGELOG.md 0.5.0 entry added between [Unreleased] and [0.4.0]. Covers the full Set 031 deliverable surface: decision_consensus schema (with V1/V1.5/V2 category split rationale), _validate_decision_consensus boundary, consensus_journal.py module (ConsensusRecord + compute_question_hash + append_record + write_full_payload + write_consensus_record + validate_record_inputs), AJV schema mirror, workflow doc section, per-agent pointers, .gitignore update, and 33 new tests (633 passed + 1 skipped vs 599 pre-set). Release notes section explicitly notes the orchestrator-side wiring is NOT in 0.5.0 (follow-on set).
pyproject.toml version 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0. Matching ai_router/__init__.py __version__ bump (kept in sync; the release workflow's tag-vs-pyproject check requires both agree on the canonical PEP 440 form).

.gitignore: added ai_router/consensus-decisions/ to exclude the default-on but disk-heavy full-payload sibling directory. The JSONL journal itself (ai_router/consensus-decisions.jsonl) is NOT gitignored - by design, follows the router-metrics.jsonl precedent so the audit trail survives across conversations. Inline comment documents the distinction.

Pre-verifier pytest sanity-check: 633 passed + 1 skipped (same as S1 disposition reported). Pre-existing test-collection gap surfaced - three test modules import migrate_router_config.py which depends on ruamel.yaml but the dep is not in pyproject.toml. The local .venv had lost the install since S1 close; ad-hoc `pip install ruamel.yaml` unblocked collection. Out-of-scope: packaging fix deferred to a future set (Set 031 is the schema set, not the packaging set).

Round-A end-of-session verification: ai_router.query(model='gemini-pro', task_type='session-verification', content=bundle). Bundle: git diff HEAD --no-color across S2 files (CHANGELOG, version files, gitignore, pointer blocks, ai-assignment) + the workflow-doc target snippet (for pointer-target check). Per memory feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling - RouteResult dumped via dataclasses.asdict + json.dumps before attribute access. Response flagged ONE Major: the CHANGELOG 'Consumer-repo notification' bullet used past tense ('each got a one-liner...') which over-claims at release time when the cross-repo writes are operator-gated AFTER PyPI publish. Cost $0.008.

Round-A Major finding addressed: CHANGELOG 'Consumer-repo notification' bullet rephrased from past-tense ('each got a one-liner...at the same time as this release') to a release-time-accurate form ('As an operator-gated step alongside this release, ... CLAUDE.md files each get a one-liner pointer ... Those edits live outside this repo's working tree; consumers can adopt the feature without them by setting delegation.decision_consensus.enabled: true in their own router-config.yaml once the orchestrator-side wiring ships'). Removes the past-tense over-claim and explicitly notes consumer adoption does not require those edits.

Round-B verification confirming the Round-A fix. Same script, ROUND=2 env var (initially failed because $env:ROUND=2 PowerShell syntax was passed to bash - $0.009 wasted on a re-run that ran as Round-A again; corrected to bash syntax ROUND=2 cmd for the actual Round-B). Round-B verdict: APPROVE. 'The CHANGELOG.md Consumer-repo notification bullet has been successfully rephrased ... accurately reflects that the cross-repo writes are an operator-gated, post-release action. The original concern is resolved.' Cost $0.007 (productive Round-B) + $0.009 (wasted env-var re-run). Total S2 verifier spend $0.024; Set 031 cumulative spend $0.044.

python -m build produced dist/dabbler_ai_router-0.5.0-py3-none-any.whl + dist/dabbler_ai_router-0.5.0.tar.gz. Wheel inspection confirmed: includes ai_router/consensus_journal.py, includes the updated ai_router/router-config.yaml, metadata Version: 0.5.0. Pre-release smoke check for what would land on PyPI.

Release path was the documented tag-driven OIDC trusted-publishing flow at .github/workflows/release.yml (per docs/planning/release-process.md) - NOT twine. The S2 spec said `python -m build && twine upload`; operator confirmed the OIDC path at the release-path decision point. Sequence: commit ebe4de2 (S2 implementation + release-readiness), push origin master, tag v0.5.0, push tag. GitHub Actions workflow 26128035426 ran classify (3s) + build (13s) + publish-to-PyPI (16s, gated on the `pypi` environment approval, which the operator granted in the GitHub UI). PyPI live at https://pypi.org/project/dabbler-ai-router/0.5.0/ (verified via pypi.org/pypi/dabbler-ai-router/json -> Latest: 0.5.0).

Byte-identical 'Decision-time consensus (pointer)' block added to dabbler-platform/CLAUDE.md and dabbler-access-harvester/CLAUDE.md, after each repo's existing 'Delegation Discipline (pointer)' section. Diff-verified byte-identical against this repo's CLAUDE.md via diff. healthcare-accessdb SKIPPED per operator decision: it's Lightweight tier and has no local docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md for the pointer to resolve against; the CHANGELOG note 'consumer adoption doesn't require those edits' covers the Lightweight case. Cross-repo edits left UNCOMMITTED in those working trees per operator's selection at the cross-repo-writes decision point ('I write all three; you commit/push' - operator handles commits in those repos on their own timeline).

Final-session change-log.md generated at docs/session-sets/031-delegation-consensus-config/change-log.md per the project_final_session_changelog_pre_close memory (close-out gate check_change_log_fresh requires it to pre-exist). Aggregates Sessions 1 + 2 into a single narrative: context, S1 narrative (open questions + 6-item shipped list + Round-A Critical fix), S2 narrative (6-item shipped list + Round-A Major fix + out-of-scope disclosures), end-state assertions, what does NOT ship in 0.5.0 (orchestrator-side wiring deferred), notable artifacts (PyPI URL, workflow run URL, design proposal, verifier reviews).
